concept_name: Faraday's Law of Induction — ε = −N dΦ/dt
source_book: NCERT Class 12 Ch.6 Electromagnetic Induction §6.3–6.5 (Magnetic flux, Faraday's law, Lenz's law) — table-of-contents reference only; teaching authored from first principles. The atomic concept: magnetic flux Φ = B·A·cosθ measures how much magnetic field threads a coil; an EMF is induced only while that flux is CHANGING, ε = −N dΦ/dt (Faraday); the minus sign is Lenz's law — the induced current opposes the change that caused it (energy conservation). Built instrument-native in the straightforward (non-Socratic) model: five motion-distinct teaching beats + one combined interactive state, each state exposing only its own contextual controls.

real_world_anchor.primary: Drop a strong magnet down a vertical copper pipe and it falls in slow motion — far slower than gravity alone. As the magnet moves, the magnetic flux through each ring of the copper changes, inducing currents in the copper; by Lenz's law those currents oppose the motion that made them, so they brake the fall. No contact, no friction — just a changing flux fighting back. That is Faraday's law and Lenz's law you can watch with your eyes.
real_world_anchor.secondary: Every transformer on India's power grid — the 220 kV, 400 kV and 765 kV step-up and step-down transformers, and the small one in a phone charger — runs on this law. An alternating current makes a changing flux in an iron core, and that changing flux induces a voltage in the second coil. An induction cooktop uses the same idea: a changing flux induces heating currents directly in the pan.

theta.name: Angle between B and the coil's area vector

N.name: Number of turns in the coil

formulas.magnetic_flux: Φ = B·A·cosθ  (weber, Wb) — how much field threads the coil
formulas.faradays_law: ε = −N dΦ/dt  — an EMF is induced only while the flux changes
formulas.lenz_law: the minus sign: the induced current opposes the change that caused it
formulas.rate_and_turns: faster flux change → bigger ε; more turns N → bigger ε

constraints: Magnetic flux Φ = B·A·cosθ measures how much magnetic field passes through the coil
A steady flux induces NO EMF — only a CHANGING flux does (ε = −N dΦ/dt)
The induced EMF is proportional to the RATE of change of flux and to the number of turns N
Lenz's law (the minus sign): the induced current flows so as to oppose the change in flux that produced it
Reversing the direction of the flux change (push in vs pull out) reverses the sign of the induced EMF
Energy is conserved: the work done against the opposing (Lenz) force becomes the electrical energy of the induced current

aha_moment.statement: Moving the magnet changes the flux, and a changing flux induces an EMF.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: As the magnet slides into the coil the flux lines pack denser and the galvanometer needle swings off zero.

STATE_1.title: Magnetic flux: how much field threads the coil

scene_composition.text:
steady flux → ε = 0
needle stays at zero

tts_sentences.text_en: A coil sits in a magnetic field. The magnetic flux is how much of the field threads through the coil — Phi equals magnetic field B times area A times cosine theta.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक coil एक magnetic field में रखी है। magnetic flux Φ यह है कि कितनी field coil में से गुज़रती है — Phi equals magnetic field B into area A into cosine theta।
tts_sentences.text_te: oka coil magnetic field lo undi. magnetic flux Φ ante coil gunda entha field velutundo — Phi equals magnetic field B into area A into cosine theta.

tts_sentences.text_en: Here a bar magnet supplies that field. Held still, the flux through the coil stays constant — the green lines threading the coil do not change.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यहाँ एक bar magnet वह field देता है। स्थिर रखने पर coil में से flux constant रहती है — coil में से गुज़रती green lines नहीं बदलतीं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ikkada oka bar magnet aa field ni istundi. kadalకుండా unte coil gunda flux constant ga untundi — coil gunda velle green lines maaravu.

tts_sentences.text_en: And a steady flux induces nothing. The galvanometer needle stays at zero. Watch what happens the moment we move the magnet.
tts_sentences.text_hi: और steady flux कुछ induce नहीं करती। galvanometer needle zero पर रहती है। देखिए जिस पल हम magnet हिलाते हैं क्या होता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: mariyu steady flux emi induce cheyadu. galvanometer needle zero daggara untundi. manam magnet ni kadipina kshanam emi jarugutundo choodandi.

misconception_watch.belief: Just having a magnet near the coil induces a current
misconception_watch.visual_counter: With the magnet held still the flux lines are steady and the galvanometer needle sits exactly at zero.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: A steady flux induces no EMF — only a CHANGING flux does.

STATE_2.title: Push the magnet in: a changing flux induces an EMF

tts_sentences.text_en: Now slide the magnet into the coil. As it comes closer, more field threads the coil, so the flux through it rises.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब magnet को coil में सरकाइए। जैसे-जैसे वह पास आता है, ज़्यादा field coil में से गुज़रती है, तो उसमें से flux बढ़ती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ippudu magnet ni coil loki jarapandi. adi daggaraki vachhే kolla, ekkuva field coil gunda velutundi, kaabatti dani gunda flux perugutundi.

tts_sentences.text_en: The instant the flux changes, the galvanometer needle swings off zero. A current has been induced — this is Faraday's law: induced EMF equals minus the rate of change of flux.
tts_sentences.text_hi: जिस पल flux बदलती है, galvanometer needle zero से हट जाती है। एक current induce हुई — यही Faraday's law है: induced EMF equals minus the rate of change of flux।
tts_sentences.text_te: flux maare kshanam, galvanometer needle zero nunchi kadulutundi. oka current induce ayindi — ide Faraday's law: induced EMF equals minus the rate of change of flux.

tts_sentences.text_en: Stop moving the magnet and the needle drops back to zero — even though the magnet is still inside. It is the CHANGE, not the presence, that matters.
tts_sentences.text_hi: magnet हिलाना बंद कीजिए और needle वापस zero पर आ जाती है — भले ही magnet अभी भी अंदर है। मायने change रखता है, presence नहीं।
tts_sentences.text_te: magnet kadapadam aapandi, needle malli zero ki vachestundi — magnet inka lopala unnaa sare. maney vishayam change, presence kaadu.

misconception_watch.belief: The size of the flux determines the induced EMF
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The needle deflects only while the magnet is moving; hold it still inside the coil and the needle returns to zero despite a large flux.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: The induced EMF depends on the RATE OF CHANGE of flux (dΦ/dt), not on the flux value itself.

STATE_3.title: Pull it out: the EMF reverses

scene_composition.text: pull out → flux falls → ε flips sign

tts_sentences.text_en: Now pull the magnet back out. This time the flux through the coil falls instead of rising.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब magnet को वापस बाहर खींचिए। इस बार coil में से flux बढ़ने के बजाय घटती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ippudu magnet ni malli bayataki laagandi. ee saari coil gunda flux peruguta badulu taggutundi.

tts_sentences.text_en: And the needle swings the opposite way. A falling flux induces an EMF of the opposite sign to a rising flux.
tts_sentences.text_hi: और needle उल्टी दिशा में झूलती है। घटती flux, बढ़ती flux के opposite sign की EMF induce करती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: mariyu needle vyatireka disha lo oogutundi. taggu flux, perigे flux ki opposite sign EMF ni induce chestundi.

tts_sentences.text_en: So the DIRECTION of the flux change sets the sign of the EMF. Push in, one way; pull out, the other. That sign is the clue to Lenz's law.
tts_sentences.text_hi: तो flux change की DIRECTION, EMF का sign तय करती है। अंदर धकेलो एक तरफ; बाहर खींचो दूसरी तरफ। वह sign Lenz's law का इशारा है।
tts_sentences.text_te: kaabatti flux change yokka DIRECTION, EMF sign ni nirnayistundi. lopaliki nettite oka vaipu; bayataki laagite maro vaipu. aa sign Lenz's law ki suchana.

misconception_watch.belief: The induced current always flows the same way whenever the magnet moves
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Pushing in swings the needle one way; pulling out swings it the opposite way.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Reversing the direction of the flux change reverses the sign of the induced EMF and current.

STATE_4.title: Lenz's law: the coil pushes back

scene_composition.text: the − sign: induced current OPPOSES the change

approach → N face repels
withdraw → S face attracts

why a magnet falls slowly
through a copper pipe

tts_sentences.text_en: Why the minus sign in Faraday's law? Lenz's law answers it: the induced current always flows so as to OPPOSE the change that created it.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Faraday's law में minus sign क्यों? Lenz's law जवाब देता है: induced current हमेशा इस तरह बहती है कि उसे बनाने वाले change का OPPOSE करे।
tts_sentences.text_te: Faraday's law lo minus sign enduku? Lenz's law samadhanam istundi: induced current eppudu daanini srustinchina change ni OPPOSE chese vidhamga pravahistundi.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the coil's face. As the magnet approaches it becomes a red N pole and repels it; pull the magnet away and it flips to a blue S pole and attracts it — always opposing the change.
tts_sentences.text_hi: coil के face को देखिए। magnet पास आते ही वह red N pole बनकर उसे repel करता है; magnet दूर खींचिए तो वह blue S pole बनकर उसे attract करता है — हमेशा change का विरोध।
tts_sentences.text_te: coil face ni choodandi. magnet daggaraki raagane adi red N pole ga maari daanini repel chestundi; magnet ni dooram laagite adi blue S pole ga maari daanini attract chestundi — eppudu change ni vyatirekistundi.

tts_sentences.text_en: This is why a magnet dropped down a copper pipe falls in slow motion, and why energy is conserved: the work done against the push-back becomes the electrical energy of the current.
tts_sentences.text_hi: इसीलिए copper pipe में गिराया magnet slow motion में गिरता है, और energy conserve होती है: push-back के खिलाफ किया काम current की electrical energy बन जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: anduke copper pipe lo veyabadina magnet slow motion lo paduthundi, mariyu energy conserve avutundi: push-back ki vyatirekamga chesina pani current yokka electrical energy avutundi.

misconception_watch.belief: The induced current could flow either way — the minus sign is just a convention
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The coil always develops the pole that repels an approaching magnet and attracts a departing one; the push-back arrow points against the motion.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Lenz's law fixes the direction: the induced current always opposes the change in flux, conserving energy.

STATE_5.title: Rate and turns: what makes the EMF bigger

scene_composition.text:
move faster → dΦ/dt bigger
→ bigger ε

tts_sentences.text_en: How big is the induced EMF? Move the magnet back and forth and change the speed. The faster the flux changes, the larger the deflection.
tts_sentences.text_hi: induced EMF कितनी बड़ी? magnet को आगे-पीछे कीजिए और speed बदलिए। flux जितनी तेज़ी से बदलती है, deflection उतना बड़ा।
tts_sentences.text_te: induced EMF entha peddadi? magnet ni munduku venakaku kadapandi mariyu speed maarchandi. flux entha vegam ga maarite, deflection antha peddadi.

tts_sentences.text_en: And the number of turns matters too. Each turn adds its own EMF, so the total is N times the rate of change: induced EMF equals minus N times d-Phi by d-t.
tts_sentences.text_hi: और turns की संख्या भी मायने रखती है। हर turn अपनी EMF जोड़ता है, तो total N into rate of change होती है: induced EMF equals minus N into d-Phi by d-t।
tts_sentences.text_te: mariyu turns sankhya kooda maney. prati turn dani sonta EMF ni kaluputundi, kaabatti total N into rate of change avutundi: induced EMF equals minus N into d-Phi by d-t.

tts_sentences.text_en: So two dials control the induced EMF: how fast you change the flux, and how many turns the coil has. That is the whole of Faraday's law.
tts_sentences.text_hi: तो दो dials induced EMF को control करती हैं: आप flux कितनी तेज़ी से बदलते हैं, और coil में कितने turns हैं। यही पूरा Faraday's law है।
tts_sentences.text_te: kaabatti rendu dials induced EMF ni control chestayi: meeru flux ni entha vegam ga maarustaru, mariyu coil lo enni turns unnayi. ide poorti Faraday's law.

misconception_watch.belief: A stronger magnet is the only way to get a bigger induced EMF
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Speeding up the same magnet, or adding turns to the coil, raises the needle deflection without changing the magnet.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Induced EMF grows with the RATE of flux change (dΦ/dt) and with the number of turns N.

STATE_6.title: Explore: move the magnet, set the turns

scene_composition.text: Move the magnet · set speed · set turns

faster or more turns → bigger ε
hold it still → ε = 0

tts_sentences.text_en: Your turn. Drag the magnet in and out, change its speed, and set how many turns the coil has.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब आपकी बारी। magnet को अंदर-बाहर खींचिए, उसकी speed बदलिए, और coil के turns set कीजिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: ippudu mee vantu. magnet ni lopaliki bayataki laagandi, dani speed maarchandi, mariyu coil turns set cheyandi.

tts_sentences.text_en: Move it faster or add turns and the induced EMF grows. Hold the magnet still and the EMF falls to zero, however deep inside it sits.
tts_sentences.text_hi: तेज़ हिलाओ या turns बढ़ाओ तो induced EMF बढ़ती है। magnet स्थिर रखो तो EMF zero हो जाती है, चाहे वह कितना भी अंदर हो।
tts_sentences.text_te: vegam ga kadapandi leda turns perigandi, induced EMF perugutundi. magnet ni kadalకుండా unte EMF zero avutundi, adi entha lopala unnaa sare.

tts_sentences.text_en: One idea ties it all together: flux is B times A, and a changing flux induces EMF equals minus N times d-Phi by d-t. That is Faraday's law of induction.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक idea सब कुछ जोड़ता है: flux is B into A, और changing flux, EMF equals minus N into d-Phi by d-t induce करती है। यही Faraday's law of induction है।
tts_sentences.text_te: oka idea anni kalipi unchutundi: flux is B into A, mariyu changing flux, EMF equals minus N into d-Phi by d-t ni induce chestundi. ide Faraday's law of induction.

STATE_1.label: Magnetic flux: Φ = B·A·cosθ
STATE_1.caption: Magnet held still → flux steady → needle at zero

STATE_2.label: Push in: a changing flux induces EMF
STATE_2.caption: Magnet slides in → flux rises → needle swings

STATE_3.label: Pull out: the EMF reverses
STATE_3.caption: Magnet slides out → flux falls → needle reverses

STATE_4.label: Lenz's law: the coil pushes back
STATE_4.caption: Approach → N face repels · withdraw → S face attracts — always opposing

STATE_5.label: Rate and turns: ε = −N dΦ/dt
STATE_5.caption: Faster change or more turns → bigger EMF

STATE_6.label: Explore: magnet, speed, turns
STATE_6.caption: Drag the magnet · set speed · set turns — watch Φ and ε
